mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers body covering (A), including general features of integument (A.1), invertebrate integuments (A.2), vertebrate integuments with skin layers and derivatives (A.3), skin variations among vertebrates (A.4), embryology and evolution (A.5), biodynamics (A.6), and human integument (A.7).

Directly covers the body skeleton (B), including roles (B.1), composition of skeletal elements from cuticular to semirigid structures (B.2), invertebrate skeleton (B.3), vertebrate skeleton (B.4), and joints (B.5).
Bone as a cuticular skeletal element (B.2.a), part of the vertebrate skeleton structure and function (B.4), with properties and development (B.6).
Skeletal muscle as body musculature (C), specifically striated muscle as a complex contractile tissue (C.2.b), within vertebrate muscle systems (C.5).
Anatomy as the study of structure underpins all three systems: body covering (A), body skeleton (B), and body musculature (C), with comparative anatomy across taxa.
The human body is specifically addressed through human integument and derivatives (A.7) and the human skeletal system (B.7).
Vertebrate integuments (A.3), the vertebrate skeleton including embryology and evolution (B.4), and vertebrate muscle systems (C.5).
Mammals have distinctive skin derivatives including hair (A.3.b) and endoskeletal features distinguishing them from other vertebrates (B.4).
Molluscs have invertebrate integuments with noncellular coatings (A.2) and skeletal elements including shells (B.3).
Biodynamics of vertebrate skin (A.6) and muscle contraction mechanisms (C.3) represent physiological function of these systems.
The heart as cardiac muscle relates to complex contractile tissues (C.2.b), though distinct from skeletal striated muscle.
Meat as predominantly muscle tissue connects to the body musculature (C), composed mainly of skeletal muscle fibers.
Birds have feathered, keratinized skin coverings as vertebrate skin derivatives and appendages (A.3.b), with lightweight skeletons adapted for flight.
